subject: A venomous Sliver creature — a hunched, lanky parasitic predator with elongated clawed limbs, exposed ribcage, and a long whiplike tail tipped with a stinger. It crouches in a dim cavernous space, jaws open in a hiss.
description: A sickly, sinewy Sliver crouches forward in a dark cave or burrow, its ribcage exposed and skin stretched tight over a hunched skeletal frame. Two long arms terminate in oversized hooked claws that brace against the rocky ground. A whip-thin tail curls behind it, ending in a barbed stinger. The creature's head is elongated and eyeless, jaws cracked open as if hissing or about to strike. The palette is almost entirely sickly greens, putrid yellows, and inky black, with the figure's pale-green sinews lit from above against deep shadow. The composition is centered, with the creature filling most of the frame and the dark cavern walls receding into murk.
foreground: hunched sliver creature with claws braced on rock, stinger-tail curled behind
background: dark cave or burrow interior, rocky walls fading into shadow
emotion: predatory aggression, hissing menace, coiled to strike
